title: Disabling strict HTTP no-referrer policy is security-sensitive
description: Disabling strict HTTP no-referrer policy is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity Hotspotcweexpress.jsHTTP header referercontains a URL set by web browsers and used by applications to track from where the user came from, it’s for instance a relevant value for web analytic services, but it can causeserious privacy and security problemsif the URL contains confidential information. Note that Firefox for instance, to prevent data leaks,removes path informationin the Referer header while browsing privately.Suppose an e-commerce website asks the user his credit card number to purchase a product:<html> <body> <form action="/valid_order" method="GET"> Type your credit card number to purchase products: <input type=text id="cc" value="1111-2222-3333-4444"> <input
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

noncompliant:
<html>
<body>
<form action="/valid_order" method="GET">
Type your credit card number to purchase products:
<input type=text id="cc" value="1111-2222-3333-4444">
<input type=submit>
</form>
</body>

checks.message: Meta referrer policy should be secure
